title: Alcohol use review with withdrawal risk and confidentiality issues

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How much are you drinking at the moment?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: About a bottle of wine most nights, sometimes more on weekends. I tried stopping and got shakes.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any seizures, hallucinations, vomiting blood, black stools, or thoughts of self-harm?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No seizures or hallucinations. No bleeding. I feel ashamed but I am not suicidal.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Are you safe at home, and do you want this shared with anyone?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: I am safe. Please do not tell my employer. My partner knows and can support me.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Because you had shakes when stopping, do not stop suddenly. We will arrange supervised reduction, thiamine, LFT/FBC, referral to alcohol counselling, and review in one week. Urgent care if confusion, seizure, hallucinations, vomiting blood, black stools, or suicidal thoughts.

candidate_notes.note: Alcohol use review: bottle wine most nights, more weekends. Prior withdrawal shakes when stopping; no seizures/hallucinations. No GI bleeding symptoms, no suicidal ideation. Safe at home, partner supportive. Confidentiality: patient does not want employer told. Plan: do not stop abruptly; supervised reduction, thiamine, LFT/FBC, alcohol counselling referral, review 1 week. Urgent advice for confusion, seizure, hallucinations, haematemesis/melaena, suicidal thoughts.
Alcohol intake above recommended limits. Advised to stop drinking and attend counselling. Partner aware.

S: Bottle of wine most nights, more weekends. Shakes when tried stopping. No seizures, hallucinations, GI bleeding, suicidal thoughts. Partner supportive; safe at home. P: supervised reduction, thiamine, bloods, alcohol counselling referral, review 1 week.

Hazardous alcohol use with withdrawal risk: shakes on stopping. No seizures/hallucinations/GI bleed/SI. Safe at home, partner support. Confidentiality preference documented: do not disclose to employer. Do not stop suddenly. Supervised reduction, thiamine, LFT/FBC, counselling referral, review 1 week. Urgent care for confusion, seizure, hallucinations, vomiting blood/melaena, SI.

CDSS alcohol pathway: daily high intake and withdrawal shakes mean abrupt cessation unsafe. Red flags denied today: seizure, hallucinations, GI bleed, suicidality. Confidentiality and supports recorded. Plan: supervised reduction, thiamine, LFT/FBC, counselling referral, review in 1 week. ED/urgent advice for withdrawal red flags or self-harm thoughts.
